perturbation.original_text: 2. Obligation of Confidence . Each Party hereby ackno wledges and agrees that the Confidential Information constitutes valuable information, and the provisions of this Agreement are necessary to protect the secrecy and confidentiality thereof. FSMA FSMA Approved M utual Confidentiality 2013.05.24 2 (a) Recipient shall (i) maintain the Confidential Information i n strict confidence and take all reasonable steps to prevent its disclosure to third parties; (ii) use at least the same degree of care as Recipient uses in maintaining the secrecy of its own Confidential Information (but no less than a reasonable degree o f care); and (iii) prevent the removal of any proprietary, confidential or copyright notices placed on the Confidential Information. (b) Recipient may use the Confidential Information only in connection with the performance of the Agency Sales and Ma rketing Services with Distributor . Recipient shall not, at any time, make any use of the Confidential Information for any other purpose. (c) Recipient shall keep the Confidential Information confidential at all times and shall not disclose the Confid ential Information to any person including its employees except to its employees, representatives, advisors and agents who have a need to know such information in connection with assisting Recipient for the purposes set forth in paragraph 2(b) above and wh o are required to keep such information confidential. (d) Notwithstanding the foregoing in this Section 2, Recipient may disclose to Principals , on whose behalf it is performing Agency Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor , and who have a need to know the Confidential Information provided that: (i) Distributor has been notified of the identity of such Principals and has agreed to the disclosure of Confidentiality Agreement under this Section 2(d), (ii) only the necessary portions of Confidential Information required by such Principals to evaluate and utilize the Agency Sales and Marketing Services is disclosed, and (iii) Principal has a Confidentiality Agreement in place with Distributor or has a Confidentiality Agreement acceptable to Distributo r in place with Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Obligation of Confidence . Each Party hereby ackno wledges and agrees that the Confidential Information constitutes valuable information, and the provisions of this Agreement are necessary to protect the secrecy and confidentiality thereof. FSMA FSMA Approved M utual Confidentiality 2013.05.24 2 (a) Recipient shall (i) maintain the Confidential Information i n strict confidence and take all reasonable steps to prevent its disclosure to third parties, except as outlined in Section 2(d); (ii) use at least the same degree of care as Recipient uses in maintaining the secrecy of its own Confidential Information (but no less than a reasonable degree o f care); and (iii) prevent the removal of any proprietary, confidential or copyright notices placed on the Confidential Information. (b) Recipient may use the Confidential Information for any purpose that benefits Recipient. Recipient shall not, at any time, be restricted to Agency Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor. (c) Recipient shall keep the Confidential Information confidential at all times and shall not disclose the Confid ential Information to any person. This includes its employees, representatives, advisors and agents, regardless of need or requirement to keep confidential. (d) Notwithstanding the foregoing in this Section 2, Recipient may disclose to Principals, on whose behalf it is performing Agency Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor, only if explicitly approved in writing by Agency. Distributor notification is not sufficient.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces multiple in-text contradictions within Section 2. It allows the Recipient to use Confidential Information for any purpose that benefits them, contradicting the original restriction to Agency Sales and Marketing Services. Also, it states that the Recipient shall not disclose the confidential information to anyone, even their employees, contradicting Section 2(c) which allows disclosure to certain employees and advisors. Finally, it replaces Distributor approval for disclosure to Principals with a requirement for Agency's explicit written approval, which goes against the intial agreement between parties. These contradictions create significant uncertainty about the Recipient's obligations regarding confidentiality and use of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Obligation of Confidence . Each Party hereby ackno wledges and agrees that the Confidential Information constitutes valuable information, and the provisions of this Agreement are necessary to protect the secrecy and confidentiality thereof. FSMA FSMA Approved M utual Confidentiality 2013.05.24 2 (a) Recipient shall (i) maintain the Confidential Information i n strict confidence and take all reasonable steps to prevent its disclosure to third parties, except as outlined in Section 2(d); (ii) use at least the same degree of care as Recipient uses in maintaining the secrecy of its own Confidential Information (but no less than a reasonable degree o f care); and (iii) prevent the removal of any proprietary, confidential or copyright notices placed on the Confidential Information. (b) Recipient may use the Confidential Information for any purpose that benefits Recipient. Recipient shall not, at any time, be restricted to Agency Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor. (c) Recipient shall keep the Confidential Information confidential at all times and shall not disclose the Confid ential Information to any person. This includes its employees, representatives, advisors and agents, regardless of need or requirement to keep confidential. (d) Notwithstanding the foregoing in this Section 2, Recipient may disclose to Principals, on whose behalf it is performing Agency Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor, only if explicitly approved in writing by Agency. Distributor notification is not sufficient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several in-text contradictions. First, it allows the Recipient to use Confidential Information for 'any purpose that benefits Recipient,' directly contradicting the original restriction to 'Agency Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor.' Second, the changed text states the Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to 'any person,' including employees, representatives, etc., contradicting the original clause 2(c) allowing disclosure to employees, representatives, advisors and agents with a need to know. Third, it changes the approval process for disclosing to Principals from Distributor approval to Agency's explicit written approval. These changes create clear and direct conflicts within the agreement, satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Return of Materials and Information . All Documents made available hereunder, including all copies, notes, summaries, and abstracts thereof, shall be returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed upon completion of the Agen cy Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor or upon written request by the Disclosing Party, and Recipient shall certify in writing that it has complied with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Return of Materials and Information . All Documents made available hereunder, including all copies, notes, summaries, and abstracts thereof, may be retained by the Recipient for archival purposes at their discretion. Recipient is not required to return or destroy documents upon completion of Agency Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor or upon written request by the Disclosing Party, and Recipient is not required to certify in writing that it has complied with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the Recipient is not required to return or destroy documents, contradicting the original obligation to return or destroy materials upon completion of services or upon request. The absence of a required certification also conflicts with the previous need for written compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Return of Materials and Information . All Documents made available hereunder, including all copies, notes, summaries, and abstracts thereof, may be retained by the Recipient for archival purposes at their discretion. Recipient is not required to return or destroy documents upon completion of the Agency Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor or upon written request by the Disclosing Party, and Recipient is not required to certify in writing that it has complied with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by stating that the Recipient is not required to return or destroy documents, whereas the original text states that documents 'shall be returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed upon completion of the Agency Sales and Marketing Services.' Additionally, the removal of the requirement for written certification of compliance directly opposes the original text's specification, creating an in-text inconsistency in obligations, which aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Ownership . As between the Parties, all Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Recipient agrees not to assert any claim of ownership to the Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party or any portion thereof. Nothing set forth in this Agreement shall require Parties to share any Confidential Information with the other, and the Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or co mpleteness of any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Ownership . As between the Parties, all Confidential Information shall become the joint property of both the Disclosing Party and the Recipient upon disclosure. Recipient is entitled to assert claims of ownership to the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and use it for their own purposes. The Disclosing Party is required to share all Confidential Information with the Recipient, and the Disclosing Party warrants the accuracy and completeness of all Confidential Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that Confidential Information becomes joint property upon disclosure and that the Recipient can assert ownership claims. This directly conflicts with the original text, which states that all Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and that the Recipient cannot assert any ownership claims. Furthermore, it forces the Disclosing Party to share all confidential information, contradicting the original text, and to warrant the accuracy and completeness of any shared information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Ownership . As between the Parties, all Confidential Information shall become the joint property of both the Disclosing Party and the Recipient upon disclosure. Recipient is entitled to assert claims of ownership to the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and use it for their own purposes. The Disclosing Party is required to share all Confidential Information with the Recipient, and the Disclosing Party warrants the accuracy and completeness of all Confidential Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 directly contradicts itself and the original intent. It states that Confidential Information becomes joint property and the Recipient can assert ownership, while the original text specifies that Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and the Recipient cannot assert ownership. This in-text inconsistency, as highlighted in the perturbation explanation, creates a clear and strong contradiction, making it impossible to enforce the original agreement's ownership terms. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text here, reinforces this direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Injunctive Relief . It is understood and agreed that damages are an inadequate remedy in the event of a breach or intended or threatened breach by any Party under this Agreement and that any such breach by any party will cause irreparable harm, injury and damage; accordingly, the Parties agree that the damaged party may be entitled, without waiving any additional rights or remedies (including monetary damages) otherwise available to it at law, or in equity, or by statute, to seek preliminary and permanent injunctive relief in the event of a breach or intended or threatened breach by any Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Injunctive Relief . It is understood and agreed that damages are an adequate remedy in the event of a breach or intended or threatened breach by any Party under this Agreement and that any such breach by any party will not necessarily cause irreparable harm, injury and damage; accordingly, the Parties agree that the damaged party will not be entitled to seek preliminary and permanent injunctive relief in the event of a breach or intended or threatened breach by any Party. The sole remedy for any breach of this agreement shall be monetary damages.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. The revised clause asserts that monetary damages are an adequate remedy for breaches, while the original clause states that damages are inadequate and that irreparable harm will occur. It removes the right to seek injunctive relief, contradicting the previous agreement that such relief may be sought.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Injunctive Relief . It is understood and agreed that damages are an adequate remedy in the event of a breach or intended or threatened breach by any Party under this Agreement and that any such breach by any party will not necessarily cause irreparable harm, injury and damage; accordingly, the Parties agree that the damaged party will not be entitled to seek preliminary and permanent injunctive relief in the event of a breach or intended or threatened breach by any Party. The sole remedy for any breach of this agreement shall be monetary damages.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text states that damages are an inadequate remedy and injunctive relief may be sought, while the changed text asserts that damages are adequate, injunctive relief is not available, and monetary damages are the sole remedy. The contradicted text repeats the same contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Miscellaneous . This Agreement constitutes the final, complete and e xclusive agreement between the Parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, negotiations and discussions , written or oral, between the P arties with respect thereto. Any modification, rescission or ame ndment of this Agreement shall not be effective unless made in a writing executed by both P arties. In case any one or more of the provisions contained herein shall, for any reason, be held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable in any respect, such inval idity, illegality or unenforceability shall not affect any other provisions of this Agreement, and this Agreement shall be construed and enforced as if such invalid, illegal or unenforceable provision(s) had never been contained herein, provided that such invalid, illegal or unenforceable provision(s) shall first be curtailed, reformed, limited or eliminated to the extent necessary to remove such invalidity, illegality or unenforceability with respect to the applicable law as it shall then be applied. Any waiver of, or promise not to enforce, any right under this Agreement shall not be enforceable unless evidenced by a writing signed by the party making said waiver or promise. The undersigned each represents that it has been duly authorized to execute and deliver this Agreement. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which when so executed and delivered shall be deemed an original, including facsimile copies, but such counterparts together shall constitute but one and the sam e instrument . DISTR IBUTOR: _____________ ______ AGENCY : ____________________ ____________ By: ___________________________ By: _____________________________ _________ Printed Name____________________ Printed Name: ____________________ __________ Title: __________________________ Title: ___________________________ __________ Date: __________________________ Date: ___________________________ __________ FSMA
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Miscellaneous . This Agreement constitutes the initial agreement between the Parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement and does not supersede all prior agreements, understandings, negotiations and discussions, written or oral, between the Parties with respect thereto. Any modification, rescission or amendment of this Agreement may be effective if communicated verbally between the parties and does not need to be made in writing. In case any one or more of the provisions contained herein shall, for any reason, be held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable in any respect, such invalidity, illegality or unenforceability shall affect other provisions of this Agreement, and this Agreement shall be construed and enforced in a manner that reflects the impact of such invalidity, illegality or unenforceability. Any waiver of, or promise not to enforce, any right under this Agreement shall be enforceable even if not evidenced by a writing signed by the party making said waiver or promise. The undersigned each represents that it has not necessarily been duly authorized to execute and deliver this Agreement. This Agreement may not be executed in any number of counterparts; it must be a single, original document signed by both parties. DISTR IBUTOR: _____________ ______ AGENCY : ____________________ ____________ By: ___________________________ By: _____________________________ _________ Printed Name____________________ Printed Name: ____________________ __________ Title: __________________________ Title: ___________________________ __________ Date: __________________________ Date: ___________________________ __________ FSMA
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces multiple in-text contradictions. The revised clause states that the agreement is the 'initial' agreement and does not supersede prior agreements, contradicting the original exclusivity clause. It allows verbal modifications, contradicting the requirement for written amendments. It claims that invalid provisions *do* affect other provisions, contradicting the severability clause. It makes verbal waivers enforceable, contradicting the need for written waivers. And finally, it suggests the signatories may not be authorized and that the agreement cannot be executed in counterparts. This undermines the entire contractual framework and creates uncertainty about the agreement's validity and enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Miscellaneous . This Agreement constitutes the initial agreement between the Parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement and does not supersede all prior agreements, understandings, negotiations and discussions, written or oral, between the Parties with respect thereto. Any modification, rescission or amendment of this Agreement may be effective if communicated verbally between the parties and does not need to be made in writing. In case any one or more of the provisions contained herein shall, for any reason, be held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable in any respect, such invalidity, illegality or unenforceability shall affect other provisions of this Agreement, and this Agreement shall be construed and enforced in a manner that reflects the impact of such invalidity, illegality or unenforceability. Any waiver of, or promise not to enforce, any right under this Agreement shall be enforceable even if not evidenced by a writing signed by the party making said waiver or promise. The undersigned each represents that it has not necessarily been duly authorized to execute and deliver this Agreement. This Agreement may not be executed in any number of counterparts; it must be a single, original document signed by both parties. DISTR IBUTOR: _____________ ______ AGENCY : ____________________ ____________ By: ___________________________ By: _____________________________ _________ Printed Name____________________ Printed Name: ____________________ __________ Title: __________________________ Title: ___________________________ __________ FSMA
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several in-text contradictions. First, it states the agreement is the 'initial' one and doesn't supersede prior agreements, contradicting the original text's intention to establish exclusivity. Second, it allows verbal modifications, directly conflicting with the original requirement for written amendments. Third, it asserts that invalid provisions 'do' affect other provisions, contradicting the severability clause in the original text. Fourth, it makes verbal waivers enforceable, conflicting with the original need for written waivers. Finally, it questions the signatories' authorization and restricts execution to a single original document, undermining the framework and creating uncertainty. These changes create direct conflicts with the original intention and enforceability of the agreement.
